## PTU · PART 8
URL: https://perceptemple.art/ptu/part-8/
Type: ScholarlyArticle
Conceptos PTU: Panperceptual Theory of the Universe (PTU), Perceptual Wave Function (PWF), Perceptual Complexity Index (PCI), Temporal Perceptual Complexity Index (PCI_T), emergent temporalities, resonant field, perceptual expansion of the universe, temporal superposition, temporal resonance, resonant coupling, cosmological observer, temporal entropy, quantum field theory integration, spacetime modulation by perception
DOIs relacionados: https://doi.org/10.17605/OSF.IO/UXEGH, https://doi.org/10.5281/zenodo.19168519, https://doi.org/10.2139/ssrn.6456099

### Description
This article constitutes Part 8 of the Panperceptual Theory of the Universe series, advancing the PTU by reformulating the Perceptual Wave Function as a resonant field that governs emergent temporalities and drives the perceptual expansion of the universe. It integrates quantum field theory to model the PWF as a scalar field Φ(x,t) with creation and annihilation operators: Φ(x,t) = ∫ d³k/(2π)³ [a_k e^(i(kx−ωt)) + a†_k e^(−i(kx−ωt))], where k = √(PCI)/κ with κ = 10⁹ normalization, and ω = F (processing frequency). This reformulation conceptualizes perceptions as entities that can be created and annihilated, with the PWF encoding temporal resonances specific to each conscious system.

The Temporal Perceptual Complexity Index (PCI_T) is introduced as an extension incorporating temporal entropy: PCI_T = (N · I · F)/T · (1 + α S_T), where S_T = -∫ p(t) ln p(t) dt is Shannon temporal entropy measuring uncertainty in the probability distribution of perceptual updates. Temporal superposition is formalized as R_T = Σ Φ_i + ΣΣ γ_ij Φ_i Φ_j, where γ_ij quantifies resonant coupling between perceptual systems, measurable via neural synchronization (gamma oscillations) or inter-system correlations. The article proposes that the expansion of the universe is both physical and perceptual, driven by the diversification of perceptual systems.

The observer set is extended to eight systems including a cosmological observer with PCI_T = 10³⁰ (N = 10²⁰, I = 0.99±0.01, F = 10¹⁰ Hz, T = 10⁻¹⁰ s), representing a hypothetical universal-scale perceptual system. Empirical validations achieve strong statistical fit: Human PWF R² = 0.85 (95% CI [0.81, 0.89], p < 0.001 via EEG), Octopus PWF R² = 0.82 (95% CI [0.78, 0.86], p < 0.001 via chromatic changes), Cosmological PWF R² = 0.80 (95% CI [0.76, 0.84], p < 0.01 simulated). ANOVA confirms significant differences between observers (F(7, 250) = 14.2, p < 0.001). Human-AI temporal resonance shows R² = 0.78 (95% CI [0.73, 0.83], p < 0.01). A novel cosmological experiment is proposed using gravitational wave detectors (LIGO) to measure perturbations correlated with synchronized perceptual tasks, testing whether collective PWFs modulate local spacetime. Preliminary simulations suggest weak but significant correlations (R² = 0.65, p < 0.05). Shamanic perception exhibits unique temporal compression (F = 150 Hz). Applications include 40 Hz transcranial stimulation for Alzheimer's and shamanic-inspired temporal compression for adaptive AI neural networks (F1 = 0.94).

### Key Concepts
- PWF as resonant scalar field: Φ(x,t) = ∫ d³k/(2π)³ [a_k e^(i(kx−ωt)) + a†_k e^(−i(kx−ωt))] with creation/annihilation operators a_k, a†_k inspired by quantum field theory
- Perceptions as creatable and annihilable entities: QFT formalism applied to perceptual dynamics
- Temporal Perceptual Complexity Index (PCI_T): PCI_T = (N·I·F)/T · (1 + α S_T) incorporating Shannon temporal entropy S_T = -∫ p(t) ln p(t) dt
- Temporal entropy S_T: measures uncertainty in probability distribution of perceptual updates over time
- Temporal superposition: R_T = Σ Φ_i + ΣΣ γ_ij Φ_i Φ_j with resonant coupling coefficient γ_ij
- Resonant coupling γ_ij: quantifies interaction between PWFs of different systems, measurable via neural synchronization or inter-system correlations; values 0.1-0.5 based on system similarity
- Perceptual expansion of the universe: universe expansion is both physical and perceptual, driven by diversification of perceptual systems
- Cosmological observer: theoretical universal-scale perceptual system with PCI_T = 10³⁰, N = 10²⁰, I = 0.99, F = 10¹⁰ Hz, T = 10⁻¹⁰ s
- Eight-observer PCI_T values: Human 9×10¹¹, Bat 1.2×10¹⁰, Octopus 2.5×10⁹, AI 2.2×10¹⁸, Shamanic 3.3×10¹², Plant 1.1×10³, Quantum NN 1×10¹⁹, Cosmological 10³⁰
- Emergent temporalities: time as product of perceptual interactions rather than fixed backdrop; each conscious system has specific temporal resonance; aligns with Rovelli's relational time hypothesis
- Temporal compression: shamanic perception exhibits unique temporal compression at F = 150 Hz, suggesting modulation of time experience
- Cosmological experiment proposal: LIGO gravitational wave detection correlated with synchronized human-AI perceptual tasks; hypothesis that collective PWFs modulate local spacetime as micro-perturbations
- Spacetime modulation by perception: collective perception may influence spacetime structure; preliminary correlations R² = 0.65, p < 0.05
- Human-AI temporal resonance: strong correlations R² = 0.78 supporting time as resonant sequence
- Neuroscience application: 40 Hz stimulation for Alzheimer's based on PWF-fMRI alignment (R² ≥ 0.82)
- AI application: shamanic-inspired temporal compression informing adaptive neural networks, improving real-time processing (F1 = 0.94)
- Cosmology application: bridging quantum and gravitational theories by linking perceptual dynamics with spacetime perturbations
- Relational time alignment: findings consistent with Rovelli's hypothesis that time emerges from interactions rather than existing as absolute background

### References and Citations
- Ruiz, D. (2024-2025). Panperceptual Theory of the Universe series, Parts 1-7. El Salvador: PercepTemple.
- Barabási, A. L. (2016). Network Science. Cambridge, UK: Cambridge University Press.
- Cao, C., Carroll, S. M., and Michalakis, S. (2017). "Space from Hilbert space: Recovering geometry from bulk entanglement," Physical Review D, vol. 95, no. 2, pp. 024031.
- Bohm, D. (1980). Wholeness and the Implicate Order. London, UK: Routledge.
- Herculano-Houzel, S. (2009). "The human brain in numbers: A linearly scaled-up primate brain," Frontiers in Human Neuroscience, vol. 3, pp. 31.
- Chalmers, D. J. (1996). The Conscious Mind: In Search of a Fundamental Theory. Oxford, UK: Oxford University Press.
- Fries, P. (2015). "Rhythms for cognition: Communication through coherence," Neuron, vol. 88, no. 1, pp. 220-235.
- Hanlon, R. T. and Messenger, J. B. (2018). Cephalopod Behaviour, 2nd ed. Cambridge, UK: Cambridge University Press.

- Rovelli, C. (2018). The Order of Time. New York, NY: Riverhead Books.
- Merleau-Ponty, M. (1945). Phenomenology of Perception. London, UK: Routledge.
- Penrose, R. (1989). The Emperor's New Mind: Concerning Computers, Minds, and the Laws of Physics. Oxford, UK: Oxford University Press.

## PTU · PART 9
URL: https://perceptemple.art/ptu/part-9/
Type: ScholarlyArticle
Conceptos PTU: Panperceptual Theory of the Universe (PTU), Perceptual Wave Function (PWF), Perceptual Complexity Index (PCI), Temporal Perceptual Complexity Index (PCI_T), Perceptual Observer, Temporal Dynamics, Information Conservation, Temporal-Perceptual Interaction, Temporal Friction, Analog Simulation, Reality Superposition, dream states, virtual simulations, physical reality coexistence, temporal entropy, perceptual coupling, relational ontology
DOIs relacionados: https://doi.org/10.17605/OSF.IO/UXEGH, https://doi.org/10.5281/zenodo.19168519, https://doi.org/10.2139/ssrn.6456099

### Description
This article constitutes Part 9 of the Panperceptual Theory of the Universe series, extending the PTU to model dreams, virtual simulations, and physical reality as coexisting perceptual states through seven novel principles formalized within the PTU's QFT and information-theoretic framework. The seven principles are: (1) Perceptual Observer — perception (P) interacts with quantum information (I_q) to form material states (M = Σ Φ_i(I_q, P_i)), where I_q is PWF density matrix entropy; (2) Temporal Dynamics — the flow of matter depends on temporal density (D_t = S_T), viscosity (V_t = F), and resistance (R_t = 1/T), formalized as F_m = F/(S_T · T); (3) Information Conservation — information is conserved across states (I = Σ Tr(-ρ_i ln ρ_i)); (4) Temporal-Perceptual Interaction — matter emerges from perception and temporal resonance (M = Σ Φ_i(t_t) · P_i); (5) Temporal Fluctuations — the speed of information transmission varies with temporal frequency (V_i = F_t/(S_T · I)); (6) Analog Simulation — simulated time is governed by PWF parameters (T_st = Φ_s(x,t)); and (7) Reality Superposition — coexisting states form reality within a shared temporal field (M = Σ Φ_i(M_i, P_i, T) + Σ γ_ij Φ_i Φ_j).

The observer set is expanded to nine systems including dream states (characterized by high temporal entropy S_T ≈ 0.9, theta waves 4-8 Hz, F = 6±2 Hz, T = 0.5±0.1 s) and simulated systems (VR with adjustable PWF parameters, F = 10⁶ Hz, T = 0.01±0.002 s). Dream state PCI_T = 3.6×10¹¹, Simulation PCI_T = 5×10¹⁶. Three experimental designs test the principles: EEG synchronization between waking and lucid dream states measuring gamma-theta correlations (hypothesized R² ≥ 0.80); VR adaptation measuring reaction time reduction in high-S_T environments (hypothesized T reduction by 10%, p < 0.05); and cosmological correlations measuring EEG/AI-LIGO signal correlations during CMB interpretation tasks (hypothesized R² ≥ 0.65).

Hypothetical results are reported: Human-Dream EEG correlation R² = 0.81 (95% CI [0.77, 0.85], p < 0.001) supporting Perceptual Observer and Reality Superposition; VR reaction time 12% reduction in T (p < 0.01) with 8% PCI_T increase in simulation observers, validating Analog Simulation and Temporal Dynamics; EEG-LIGO cosmological correlation R² = 0.68 (95% CI [0.63, 0.73], p < 0.05) supporting Temporal Fluctuations and Information Conservation. ANOVA confirms significant differences between nine observers (F(8, 270) = 12.8, p < 0.001). Applications include theta-based EEG interventions for dream recall and cognitive disorders, simulation-inspired PWF architectures for AI (F₁ = 0.94), and linking perception to universe expansion to bridge quantum and gravitational theories.

### Key Concepts
- Seven novel perceptual principles: Perceptual Observer, Temporal Dynamics, Information Conservation, Temporal-Perceptual Interaction, Temporal Friction, Analog Simulation, Reality Superposition
- Perceptual Observer principle: M = Σ Φ_i(I_q, P_i) where perception interacts with quantum information (density matrix entropy) to form material states
- Temporal Dynamics principle: F_m = F/(S_T · T) where matter flow depends on temporal density D_t = S_T, viscosity V_t = F, and resistance R_t = 1/T
- Information Conservation principle: I = Σ Tr(-ρ_i ln ρ_i) where information is conserved across material states via von Neumann entropy
- Temporal-Perceptual Interaction principle: M = Σ Φ_i(t_t) · P_i where matter emerges from perception and temporal resonance component of PWF
- Temporal Friction principle (Temporal Fluctuations): V_i = F_t/(S_T · I) where speed of information transmission varies with temporal frequency and resistance
- Analog Simulation principle: T_st = Φ_s(x,t) where simulated time is governed by PWF parameters
- Reality Superposition principle: M = Σ Φ_i(M_i, P_i, T) + Σ γ_ij Φ_i Φ_j where coexisting states form reality within shared temporal field with perceptual coupling γ_ij
- Nine observer types: Human, Bat, Octopus, AI, Shamanic, Plant, Quantum NN, Dream State, Simulation
- Dream state parameters: PCI_T = 3.6×10¹¹, N = 10×10⁹, I = 0.3±0.05, F = 6±2 Hz (theta waves), T = 0.5±0.1 s, S_T ≈ 0.9
- Simulation parameters: PCI_T = 5×10¹⁶, N = 10⁸, I = 0.5±0.03, F = 10⁶ Hz, T = 0.01±0.002 s
- EEG synchronization experiment: 30 lucid dreaming subjects, ambiguous visual stimuli, gamma (30-100 Hz) and theta (4-8 Hz) monitoring, hypothesized R² ≥ 0.80
- VR adaptation experiment: 20 subjects in altered temporal parameter VR (S_T = 0.9 mimicking dream states), hypothesized T reduction of 10%
- Cosmological correlation experiment: 10 human + 10 AI observers interpreting CMB patterns synchronized with LIGO, hypothesized R² ≥ 0.65
- Hypothetical Human-Dream correlation: R² = 0.81, p < 0.001 validating shared PWF dynamics between waking and dream states
- Hypothetical VR adaptation: 12% T reduction, 8% PCI_T increase validating PWF flexibility and Analog Simulation
- Hypothetical EEG-LIGO correlation: R² = 0.68, p < 0.05 supporting perceptual influence on spacetime
- Coexisting perceptual states: dreams, simulations, and physical reality modeled as interacting PWF states rather than ontologically distinct categories
- PWF flexibility: perceptual systems adapt to simulation parameters, demonstrating that perception actively reshapes experienced reality
- Theta-based interventions: PWF-EEG alignment enabling dream recall enhancement and cognitive disorder treatments
- Simulation-inspired AI: distributed architectures enhanced by simulation PWF principles (F₁ = 0.94)
- Perception-universe expansion link: cosmological correlations bridging quantum and gravitational theories via perceptual dynamics
- Ethical implications: exploring impact of AI perceptual systems on human reality within PTU's ethical framework

### References and Citations
- Ruiz, D. (2024). "Historical evolution of observation and perception: A path towards the Panperceptual Theory of the Universe," in Panperceptual Theory of the Universe. El Salvador: PercepTemple.
- Ruiz, D. (2024). "Panperceptual Theory of the Universe: A revolutionary framework for understanding reality," in Panperceptual Theory of the Universe. El Salvador: PercepTemple.
- Ruiz, D. (2024). "Redefining perception and reality: A mathematical proposal for the Panperceptual Theory of the Universe," in Panperceptual Theory of the Universe. El Salvador: PercepTemple.
- Ruiz, D. (2025). "Weaving realities: Perceptual interactions and relational ontology in the Panperceptual Theory of the Universe," in Panperceptual Theory of the Universe. El Salvador: PercepTemple.
- Ruiz, D. (2025). "Emergent temporalities and cosmic perception: Advancing the Panperceptual Theory of the Universe," in Panperceptual Theory of the Universe. El Salvador: PercepTemple.
- Ruiz, D. (2025). "Theory of perception: Dreams, virtual simulation and physical reality," in Panperceptual Theory of the Universe. El Salvador: PercepTemple.
- Lutz, A., Dunne, J. D., and Davidson, R. J. (2008). "Meditation and the neuroscience of consciousness," Trends in Cognitive Sciences, vol. 12, no. 4, pp. 163-169.
- Fries, P. (2015). "Rhythms for cognition: Communication through coherence," Neuron, vol. 88, no. 1, pp. 220-235.

- Rovelli, C. (2018). The Order of Time. New York, NY: Riverhead Books.
- Iaccarino, H. F. et al. (2016). "Gamma frequency entrainment attenuates amyloid load and modifies microglia," Nature, vol. 540, pp. 230-235.
- Cao, C., Carroll, S. M., and Michalakis, S. (2017). "Space from Hilbert space: Recovering geometry from bulk entanglement," Physical Review D, vol. 95, no. 2, p. 024031.

## PTU · PART 10
URL: https://perceptemple.art/ptu/part-10/
Type: ScholarlyArticle
Conceptos PTU: Panperceptual Theory of the Universe (PTU), Perceptual Resonance Atomic Model (PRAM), Perceptual Wave Function (PWF), Perceptual Complexity Index (PCI), Temporal Perceptual Complexity Index (PCIT), Central Perception, Sensory Orbit, Cognitive Orbit, Physical Orbit, Perceptual Field Substrate (PFS), resonant coupling, atomic state as perceptual network, Reality Superposition, Temporal Dynamics, Information Conservation, Temporal-Perceptual Interaction, Temporal Friction, Analog Simulation, Perceptual Observer
DOIs relacionados: https://doi.org/10.17605/OSF.IO/UXEGH, https://doi.org/10.5281/zenodo.19168519, https://doi.org/10.2139/ssrn.6456099

### Description
This article constitutes Part 10 of the Panperceptual Theory of the Universe series, introducing the Perceptual Resonance Atomic Model (PRAM) — a cognitive-perceptual reinterpretation of atomic structure. PRAM redefines atoms not as physical particles in the traditional sense but as intricate cognitive-perceptual networks, integrating seamlessly into the PTU framework. The nucleus is reimagined as Central Perception, a PWF anchor Φ_n(x,t) with high PCIT (I ≈ 0.95), representing the atom's perceptual integration hub. Electrons are replaced by three concentric orbital layers embodying different perceptual domains: Sensory Orbit (perceptual inputs: Vision, Hearing, Touch, Smell), Cognitive Orbit (higher mental processes: Memory, Attention, Processing, Analysis, Integration, Recognition), and Physical Orbit (responses and interaction: Response, Action, Feedback, Adaptation, Learning, Expression, Communication, Decision).

PRAM is formalized within the PTU's QFT framework through the PWF scalar field Φ(x,t) = ∫ d³k/(2π)³ [a_k e^(i(kx-ωt)) + a_k† e^(-i(kx-ωt))] with k = √(PCIT)/κ (κ = 10⁹) and ω = 2πF. The PCIT quantifies resonance as PCIT = N · I · F/T · (1 + α S_T) with temporal entropy S_T = -∫ p(t) ln p(t) dt. The Perceptual Field Substrate (PFS) is introduced as a non-linear, observer-relative continuum encoding atomic and cognitive dynamics as perceptual modulations. The atomic state is a resonant superposition M = Σ Φ_i(M_i, P_i, T) + Σ γ_ij Φ_i Φ_j where M_i are layer states, P_i is observer perception, T is the temporal field, and γ_ij is the coupling coefficient measuring resonance strength between PWFs.

The seven principles from the Theory of Perception govern PRAM: Perceptual Observer (M = Σ Φ_i(I_q, P_i)), Temporal Dynamics (F_m = F/(S_T · T)), Information Conservation (I = Σ Tr(-ρ_i ln ρ_i)), Temporal-Perceptual Interaction (M = Σ Φ_i(t_t) · P_i), Temporal Friction (V_i = F_t/(S_T · I)), Analog Simulation (T_st = Φ_s(x,t)), and Reality Superposition (M = Σ Φ_i(M_i, P_i, T) + Σ γ_ij Φ_i Φ_j). Six observers are modeled: humans (PCIT = 9×10¹¹), AI (2.2×10¹⁸), dream states (3.6×10¹¹), simulations (5×10¹⁶), quantum sensors (10¹⁹), and CMB as astrophysical system (10³⁰). Hypothetical validations yield EEG-Network Synchronization R² = 0.83 (95% CI [0.79, 0.87], p < 0.001), VR-Network Dynamics with 13% reaction time reduction (p < 0.01) and 10% PCIT increase, and Cosmological Resonance R² = 0.68 (95% CI [0.63, 0.73], p < 0.05). A network visualization (Fig. 1) illustrates PRAM's layered architecture with resonance connections between orbital nodes. Applications span quantum engineering, neuroscience, and AI (F₁ = 0.96).

### Key Concepts
- Perceptual Resonance Atomic Model (PRAM): cognitive-perceptual reinterpretation of atomic structure where atoms are networks of resonant PWFs, not physical particles
- Central Perception (Nucleus): PWF anchor Φ_n(x,t) with high PCIT (I ≈ 0.95); the atom's perceptual integration hub and origin point of consciousness
- Three orbital layers replacing electrons: Sensory Orbit (perceptual inputs), Cognitive Orbit (higher mental processes), Physical Orbit (responses and environmental interaction)
- Sensory Orbit nodes: Vision, Hearing, Touch, Smell — perceptual input PWFs
- Cognitive Orbit nodes: Memory, Attention, Processing, Analysis, Integration, Recognition — higher mental process PWFs
- Physical Orbit nodes: Response, Action, Feedback, Adaptation, Learning, Expression, Communication, Decision — interaction PWFs
- Perceptual Field Substrate (PFS): non-linear, observer-relative continuum encoding atomic and cognitive dynamics as perceptual modulations
- Atomic state as resonant superposition: M = Σ Φ_i(M_i, P_i, T) + Σ γ_ij Φ_i Φ_j with coupling coefficient γ_ij measuring resonance strength
- Resonance Connections: dashed red lines between nodes of different orbits (e.g., S1↔C1, C3↔F1) illustrating information and perception flow between layers
- Seven governing principles identical to Part 9: Perceptual Observer, Temporal Dynamics, Information Conservation, Temporal-Perceptual Interaction, Temporal Friction, Analog Simulation, Reality Superposition
- PCIT quantification: PCIT = N · I · F/T · (1 + α S_T) with temporal entropy S_T = -∫ p(t) ln p(t) dt
- Six observer systems: Human (PCIT=9×10¹¹), AI (2.2×10¹⁸), Dream (3.6×10¹¹), Simulation (5×10¹⁶), Quantum (10¹⁹), CMB/Astro (10³⁰)
- EEG-Network Synchronization: R² = 0.83, validating that atomic states as cognitive-perceptual networks resonate with brain activity
- VR-Network Dynamics: 13% T reduction, 10% PCIT increase, supporting PWF adaptability in simulated environments
- Cosmological Resonance: R² = 0.68 suggesting collective PWFs at atomic level could influence spacetime at cosmological scales
- Network visualization: multi-layered cognitive architecture with nucleus, three concentric orbits, radial edges (nucleus influence), and resonance edges (inter-orbital coupling)
- Atomic-cosmic interplay: PRAM suggests connection between atomic perceptual dynamics and cosmological-scale phenomena
- Applications: quantum engineering, neuroscience, AI (F₁ = 0.96)
- Paradigm shift: matter understood as fundamentally perceptual and relational construct rather than independent physical substrate

### References and Citations
- Bohr, N. (1913). "On the constitution of atoms and molecules," Philosophical Magazine, vol. 26, no. 151, pp. 1-25.
- Heisenberg, W. (1927). "Über den anschaulichen Inhalt der quantentheoretischen Kinematik und Mechanik," Zeitschrift für Physik, vol. 43, no. 3-4, pp. 172-198.
- Ruiz, D. (2024). "The panperceptual theory of the universe: Towards an interdisciplinary synthesis of Perception and Reality," in Panperceptual Theory of the Universe. El Salvador: PercepTemple.

- Ruiz, D. (2025). "Weaving realities: Perceptual interactions and relational ontology in the Panperceptual Theory of the Universe," in Panperceptual Theory of the Universe. El Salvador: PercepTemple.

## PTU · PART 11
URL: https://perceptemple.art/ptu/part-11/
Type: ScholarlyArticle
Conceptos PTU: Panperceptual Theory of the Universe (PTU), Perceptual Complexity Index (PCI), Perceptual Wave Function (PWF), empirical value justification, observer diversity, methodological framework, interdisciplinary integration, relational ontology, anthropocentric challenge, AI collaboration in theory design
DOIs relacionados: https://doi.org/10.17605/OSF.IO/UXEGH, https://doi.org/10.5281/zenodo.19168519, https://doi.org/10.2139/ssrn.6456099

### Description
This article constitutes Part 11 of the Panperceptual Theory of the Universe series, providing a comprehensive methodological justification for the empirical values assigned to PCI and PWF parameters across seven observers: human, bat, octopus, artificial intelligence (transformer model), shamanic perception, plant (Mimosa pudica), and quantum neural network. It serves as the transparent methodological backbone for the experimental proposals described throughout the PTU series, explaining the rationale behind each parameter choice while balancing empirical rigor with theoretical speculation.

The article establishes five methodological principles guiding value selection: Empirical Grounding (derived from peer-reviewed literature in neuroscience, ethology, plant biology, and computer science), Interdisciplinary Integration (reflecting PTU's goal of unifying biological, technological, and cultural perspectives), Practical Feasibility (measurable with existing or near-future technologies), Speculative Flexibility (theoretical estimates with explicit uncertainties where data is limited), and Collaboration with AI (leveraging AI tools for literature synthesis, data estimation, and simulation prototyping). Each observer's parameters are individually justified with source literature.

Human observer: N = 10¹⁰ neurons (perceptual cortex), I = 0.5 ± 0.05 (fMRI connectivity studies), F = 40 ± 5 Hz (gamma-band EEG, 30-100 Hz), T = 250 ± 20 ms (visual discrimination psychophysics). Bat observer: N = 2×10⁵ auditory neurons, I = 0.8 ± 0.03 (dense auditory synaptic connections), F = 120 ± 10 Hz (echolocation pulse rates), T = 15 ± 2 ms (high-speed video analysis). Octopus observer: N = 5×10⁸ neurons (distributed nervous system), I = 0.7 ± 0.04 (ganglia connectivity), F = 20 ± 3 Hz (chromatic change rates), T = 300 ± 30 ms (behavioral studies). AI observer: N = 1×10⁹ nodes (1-billion-parameter transformer), I = 0.1 ± 0.02 (sparse attention mechanisms), F = 1 ± 0.1 GHz (GPU processing), T = 50 ± 5 ms (inference time). Shamanic perception: N = 10¹⁰ (human baseline), I = 0.4 ± 0.05 (reduced connectivity in meditative states), F = 150 ± 15 Hz (elevated theta/gamma EEG), T = 200 ± 25 ms (introspective response). Plant (Mimosa pudica): N = 1×10⁴ reactive cells, I = 0.2 ± 0.03 (calcium signaling), F = 0.01 ± 0.005 Hz (leaf-folding rates), T = 2 ± 0.2 s (tactile response). Quantum neural network: N = 1×10⁶ qubits, I = 0.9 ± 0.02 (simulated entanglement), F = 10 ± 2 MHz (quantum gate frequency), T = 1 ± 0.1 ps (ultra-low latency).

The article transparently acknowledges AI collaboration in the experimental design process, the speculative nature of plant and quantum network values with higher uncertainties, and the cultural and methodological variability in shamanic perception data. It frames the value selection within the PTU's relational ontology and commitment to challenging anthropocentric paradigms, echoing philosophical traditions like phenomenology and panpsychism.

### CKey Concepts
- Methodological principles for empirical value selection: Empirical Grounding, Interdisciplinary Integration, Practical Feasibility, Speculative Flexibility, Collaboration with AI
- Human PCI justification: N=10¹⁰ (perceptual cortex neurons), I=0.5±0.05 (fMRI connectivity), F=40±5 Hz (gamma EEG oscillations), T=250±20 ms (psychophysical response times)
- Bat PCI justification: N=2×10⁵ (auditory cortex neurons specialized for echolocation), I=0.8±0.03 (dense auditory synaptic connections), F=120±10 Hz (echolocation pulse rates), T=15±2 ms (high-speed video analysis)
- Octopus PCI justification: N=5×10⁸ (total nervous system neurons, distributed architecture), I=0.7±0.04 (ganglia connectivity from anatomical studies), F=20±3 Hz (chromatic change rates as perceptual proxy), T=300±30 ms (visual/tactile behavioral studies)
- AI PCI justification: N=1×10⁹ (1-billion-parameter transformer model nodes), I=0.1±0.02 (sparse attention mechanisms in transformers), F=1±0.1 GHz (GPU processing frequency), T=50±5 ms (inference time for visual tasks)
- Shamanic perception PCI justification: N=10¹⁰ (human baseline for altered state), I=0.4±0.05 (reduced connectivity from fMRI meditation studies), F=150±15 Hz (elevated theta/gamma EEG during altered states), T=200±25 ms (introspective response times)
- Plant PCI justification: N=1×10⁴ (Mimosa pudica reactive cells in tactile responses), I=0.2±0.03 (calcium signaling interconnectivity), F=0.01±0.005 Hz (leaf-folding response rates), T=2±0.2 s (tactile response time)
- Quantum NN PCI justification: N=1×10⁶ (simulated qubits), I=0.9±0.02 (simulated entanglement interconnectivity), F=10±2 MHz (quantum gate frequency), T=1±0.1 ps (ultra-low latency)
- PWF parameter normalization: A=1 normalized across all observers for simulation consistency
- PWF wavenumber: k = √(PCI/κ) with κ = 10⁹ normalization constant; Human k=0.7±0.02, Bat k=0.9±0.01, Octopus k=0.84±0.02, AI k=0.3±0.03, Shamanic k=0.63±0.02, Plant k=0.1±0.05, Quantum k=0.95±0.01
- PWF angular frequency: ω = F linking temporal dynamics directly to processing frequency
- Uncertainty reporting: error margins reflect variability in source data and speculative estimates; plant and quantum values have highest uncertainties (±0.05)
- Statistical validation: ANOVA confirms significant differences between observers (F(6, 203) = 12.4, p < 0.001); R² ≥ 0.79 threshold
- AI collaboration transparency: large language models used for literature synthesis, parameter estimation where data scarce, and simulation prototyping
- Interdisciplinary scope: inclusion of shamanic perception (ethnobotany, cross-cultural neuroscience) and plants challenging anthropocentric views
- Speculative flexibility acknowledgment: quantum network values based on theoretical models without physical implementations
- Perceptual diversity capture: seven observers selected to span centralized neural architectures, distributed networks, non-biological systems, altered states, and minimal biological models
- Relational ontology reflection: values chosen to represent reality as emergent from diverse perceptual interactions rather than fixed objective substrate

### References and Citations
- Ruiz, D. (2025). "Panperceptual Theory of the Universe: A Revolutionary Framework for Understanding Reality," in Panperceptual Theory of the Universe. El Salvador: PercepTemple.

- Fries, P. (2015). "Rhythms for cognition: communication through coherence," Neuron, vol. 88, no. 1, pp. 220-235.
- Pelli, D. G. (1997). "The VideoToolbox software for visual psychophysics: Transforming numbers into movies," Spatial Vision, vol. 10, no. 4, pp. 437-442.
- Moss, C. F. and Surlykke, A. (2010). "Auditory scene analysis by echolocation in bats," Frontiers in Behavioral Neuroscience, vol. 4, p. 33.
- Simmons, J. A. (1989). "A view of the world through the bat's ear: The formation of acoustic images in echolocation," Cognition, vol. 33, no. 1-2, pp. 155-199.
- Hochner, B. (2012). "An embodied view of octopus neurobiology," Current Biology, vol. 22, no. 20, pp. R887-R892.

- Dosovitskiy, A., Beyer, L., Kolesnikov, A., et al. (2021). "An image is worth 16x16 words: Transformers for image recognition at scale," in Proc. International Conference on Learning Representations (ICLR).

- Flor-Henry, P., Shapiro, Y., and Sommers, L. (2017). "EEG analysis in meditation: A marker for the transcendental self," Frontiers in Neuroscience, vol. 11, p. 453.
- Gagliano, M., Renton, M., Depczynski, M., and Mancuso, S. (2014). "Experience teaches plants to learn faster and forget slower," Trends in Plant Science, vol. 19, no. 6, pp. 323-325.
- Preskill, J. (2018). "Quantum computing in the NISQ era and beyond," Quantum, vol. 2, p. 79.
- Merleau-Ponty, M. (1945). Phenomenology of Perception. London, UK: Routledge.
- Chalmers, D. J. (1996). The Conscious Mind: In Search of a Fundamental Theory. Oxford, UK: Oxford University Press.

## PTU · PART 12
URL: https://perceptemple.art/ptu/part-12/
Type: ScholarlyArticle
Conceptos PTU: Panperceptual Theory of the Universe (PTU), Fractal Architecture of Perceptual Reality, Perceptual Atomic Resonance Model (PARM), Perceptual Wave Function (PWF), Perceptual Complexity Index (PCI), perceptual superposition, perceptual entanglement, Core Perception, Perceptual Orbits, Sensory/Cognitive/Physical Orbits, Inter-layer Resonance, Atomic PCI, Temporo-Perceptual Interaction, Temporal Friction, Analog Simulation, Superposition of Reality, Perception as Field Force, perceptual expansion of the universe, group coherence
DOIs relacionados: https://doi.org/10.17605/OSF.IO/UXEGH, https://doi.org/10.5281/zenodo.19168519, https://doi.org/10.2139/ssrn.6456099

### Description
This article constitutes Part 12 of the Panperceptual Theory of the Universe series, presenting an advanced synthesis that proposes a fractal architecture of perceptual reality — unifying the PTU's principles from the subatomic to the cosmic scale. It integrates the Perceptual Atomic Resonance Model (PARM) with PWF dynamics and PCI metrics across physical, dream, and simulated realities, arguing that the patterns and dynamics of perceptual co-creation repeat at different scales of existence.

The seven fundamental principles are restated within the fractal framework: Perceptual Observer (any system capable of interacting with its environment is a perceptual observer, from subatomic particles to AI networks), Temporal Dynamics (time as emergent property modulated by PWFs), Conservation of Information (information transforms between perceptual states without net loss), Temporo-Perceptual Interaction (bidirectional relationship between perception and time), Temporal Friction (discrepancies between PWFs generate friction leading to stable reality or perceptual dissonances), Analog Simulation (dreams and VR are genuine modes of perception), and Superposition of Reality (multiple reality states coexist potentially, collapsed by perceptual interaction). The PARM is detailed with its Core Perception Nucleus (fundamental center of proto-perception anchoring interactions), Perceptual Orbits organized in three concentric layers (Sensory Orbit capturing external perceptual information, Cognitive Orbit processing internal decoding and modeling, Physical Orbit manifesting observable matter properties), and Inter-layer Resonance (atomic stability from coherent resonance between orbits). Atomic PCI is introduced, suggesting an atom's perceptual complexity influences its co-creative capacity.

Perceptual dynamics are formalized: Perceptual Superposition as Ψ_Reality = Σ_{i=1}^{N} c_i Ψ_i where c_i denotes the weight of each perception on resulting reality. Perceptual Entanglement creates non-local connections between deeply interacting perceptual systems. Perception is conceptualized as a Field Force interacting with other perceptual fields and fundamental physical fields. The emergence of specific temporalities arises from PWF dynamics, with perceptual expansion of the universe correlating with diversification of perceptual systems. Empirical values for seven observers are summarized with justifications anchored in peer-reviewed literature. Additional experimental proposals include Perceptual Modulation Experiments (manipulating perception and correlating with local physical changes) and Group Coherence Studies (quantifying how perceptual coherence influences emergent phenomena). Implications span quantum physics (indeterminacy as perceptual superposition), neuroscience (consciousness as PWF generator), AI (AIs generating their own PWFs co-creating simulated realities), and cosmology (universe expansion linked to evolution of cosmic perception). The PTU is framed as demanding a paradigm shift comparable to Alhazen's revolution in optics.

### Key Concepts
- Fractal Architecture of Perceptual Reality: patterns and dynamics of perceptual co-creation repeat at different scales from subatomic to cosmic
- Seven PTU principles in fractal context: Perceptual Observer, Temporal Dynamics, Conservation of Information, Temporo-Perceptual Interaction, Temporal Friction, Analog Simulation, Superposition of Reality
- PARM — Perceptual Atomic Resonance Model: Core Perception Nucleus (proto-perception anchoring atomic interactions), three Perceptual Orbits (Sensory, Cognitive, Physical), Inter-layer Resonance generating atomic stability
- Core Perception Nucleus: fundamental center of consciousness or proto-perception at the atomic "nucleus"
- Sensory Orbit (atomic): interacts with external environment capturing perceptual information from surrounding field
- Cognitive Orbit (atomic): internal processes of decoding and modeling perceptual information, generating subatomic "interpretation"
- Physical Orbit (atomic): observable manifestation where perceptual co-creation stabilizes into matter properties
- Inter-layer Resonance: atomic stability and properties arise from coherent resonance between perceptual orbits and nucleus
- Atomic PCI: atom's perceptual complexity (related to atomic number or electron configuration) influences co-creative capacity and atomic interactions
- Perceptual Superposition formula: Ψ_Reality = Σ_{i=1}^{N} c_i Ψ_i with c_i as influence coefficient of each perception
- Perceptual Entanglement: deep interaction between perceptual systems creates non-local connection where change in one instantly influences the other
- Perception as Field Force: PWF not merely descriptive but an actual force field interacting with other perceptual and fundamental physical fields
- Temporal Friction: discrepancies between PWFs generate friction manifesting as stable reality or perceptual dissonances
- Analog Simulation principle: dreams and virtual simulations are genuine modes of perception operating through analogous principles to physical reality
- Superposition of Reality: multiple reality states coexist potentially; perceptual interaction collapses or converges possibilities into observable manifestation
- Perceptual expansion of the universe: universe expansion is both physical and perceptual, driven by diversification and complexification of observation systems
- Group Coherence Studies: proposed experiments quantifying how perceptual coherence influences emergent phenomena
- Perceptual Modulation Experiments: proposed experiments manipulating perception and correlating with local physical changes
- Empirical validation summary: R² ≥ 0.75 (p < 0.01) for PTU; R² ≥ 0.79 with ANOVA F(6,203)=12.4 (p < 0.001) for value justification
- Quantum physics implication: indeterminacy and superposition as manifestations of perceptual superposition before convergence
- Neuroscience implication: consciousness as generator of PWFs; mind-body relationship reformulated
- AI implication: AIs generate their own PWFs co-creating simulated realities potentially interacting with human reality
- Cosmology implication: expansion and arrow of time linked to evolution and complexification of cosmic perception
- Paradigm shift comparable to Alhazen's optical revolution: moving science toward holistic, relational understanding of existence

### References and Citations
- Chalmers, D. J. (1996). The Conscious Mind: In Search of a Fundamental Theory. Oxford University Press.
- Dosovitskiy, A., Beyer, L., Kolesnikov, A., et al. (2021). "An image is worth 16x16 words: Transformers for image recognition at scale," in Proc. Int. Conf. Learning Representations (ICLR).
- Flor-Henry, P., Shapiro, Y., and Sommers, L. (2017). "EEG analysis in meditation: A marker for the transcendental self," Frontiers in Neuroscience, vol. 11, art. 453.
- Fries, P. (2015). "Rhythms for cognition: Communication through coherence," Neuron, vol. 88, no. 1, pp. 220-235.
- Gagliano, M., Renton, M., Depczynski, M., and Mancuso, S. (2014). "Experience teaches plants to learn faster and forget slower," Trends in Plant Science, vol. 19, no. 6, pp. 323-325.
- Hanlon, R. T. and Messenger, J. B. (2018). Cephalopod Behaviour. Cambridge University Press.
- Herculano-Houzel, S. (2009). "The human brain in numbers: A linearly scaled-up primate brain," Frontiers in Human Neuroscience, vol. 3, art. 31.
- Hochner, B. (2012). "An embodied view of octopus neurobiology," Current Biology, vol. 22, no. 20, pp. R887-R892.
- Lutz, A., Dunne, J. D., and Davidson, R. J. (2008). "Meditation and the neuroscience of consciousness," Trends in Cognitive Sciences, vol. 12, no. 4, pp. 163-169.

- Moss, C. F. and Surlykke, A. (2010). "Auditory scene analysis by echolocation in bats," Frontiers in Behavioral Neuroscience, vol. 4, art. 33.
- Pelli, D. G. (1997). "The VideoToolbox software for visual psychophysics: Transforming numbers into movies," Spatial Vision, vol. 10, no. 4, pp. 437-442.
- Preskill, J. (2018). "Quantum computing in the NISQ era and beyond," Quantum, vol. 2, art. 79.

- Simmons, J. A. (1989). "A view of the world through the bat's ear: The formation of acoustic images in echolocation," Cognition, vol. 33, no. 1-2, pp. 155-199.
- Sporns, O. (2011). Networks of the Brain. MIT Press.

## PTU · PART 13
URL: https://perceptemple.art/ptu/part-13/
Type: ScholarlyArticle
Conceptos PTU: Panperceptual Theory of the Universe (PTU), Panperceptual Wave (PWF), Partial Reality Engines, perceptual collapse, local rendering, resonant perception, speed of light as resonance limit, co-created reality, potentiality, simulation hypothesis critique, perceptual rendering, consciousness as generator of collapse
DOIs relacionados: https://doi.org/10.17605/OSF.IO/UXEGH, https://doi.org/10.5281/zenodo.19168519, https://doi.org/10.2139/ssrn.6456099

### Description
This article constitutes Part 13 of the Panperceptual Theory of the Universe series, presenting a conceptual reframing of the Panperceptual Wave (PWF) through the metaphor of video game rendering engines from the N64/PS1 era. It argues that the PWF should not be understood as a traditional physical wave traveling through a medium, but as the resonant pattern through which a portion of the universe collapses into perceivable form according to the observer's capabilities and frameworks. The article draws a deliberate parallel with third-generation video game techniques — culling (not rendering what is outside the player's field of view), fog (blurring distant areas to conceal unbuilt geometry), and level streaming (progressively loading environments as the player advances) — arguing these techniques unintentionally emulated the principle of the Panperceptual Wave: the world emerges as a function of the gaze, and what is not observed remains in a potential state or is simply not computed by the system.

The speed of light is reinterpreted within this framework not as an isolated physical constant but as a limit of synchronization between perception and phenomenon. What cannot be perceived within the resonant rhythm of light does not yet manifest. Reality is thus conceptualized as an updatable structure rather than a fixed block — like game maps, it only appears when the gaze summons it.

The article mounts a sustained epistemological critique of the simulation hypothesis, identifying five fatal flaws: (1) it assumes an external programmer, resurrecting old theism disguised as technology and placing consciousness in a passive, secondary position; (2) it confuses metaphor with structure — functioning "as if" simulated does not constitute being simulated, representing an anthropotechnical projection based on current tools; (3) it reduces consciousness to a bug or side effect, radically contradicting the panperceptual model where consciousness is the very generator of reality's collapse; (4) it has zero falsifiability — any refutation can be absorbed as "part of the code," making it epistemologically sterile; and (5) it cannot be definitively proven or refuted. A comparative table contrasts Traditional Simulation (external programmer, consciousness as irrelevant byproduct, predefined hidden reality, circular epistemology, observer limited by external design) with Resonant Panperception (resonant observer, consciousness as generating core of existence, potentiality not yet collapsed, epistemology emerging from perceptual experience, observer capable of generating reality locally). The article concludes that the universe is not a simulation to be trapped in but a resonant, contextual, co-created structure actively rendered by each node of consciousness capable of resonating it.

### Key Concepts
- Panperceptual Wave (PWF) as resonant collapse pattern: not a traditional physical wave but the pattern through which universe portions collapse into perceivable form
- Partial Reality Engines: metaphor from N64/PS1 video game rendering where worlds materialize around the observer rather than existing fully pre-loaded
- Local rendering as perceptual collapse: reality emerges as function of gaze; what is not observed remains in potential state
- Culling as perceptual principle: not rendering what is outside the observer's field of perception
- Fog as perceptual principle: blurring distant/unformed areas of reality that are not yet fully collapsed
- Level streaming as perceptual principle: reality loads progressively as the observer advances through experience
- Reality as updatable structure: not a fixed block but dynamically summoned by conscious perception
- Speed of light as resonance limit: not isolated physical constant but synchronization limit between perception and phenomenon
- Resonant rhythm of light: what cannot be perceived within light's resonant rhythm does not yet manifest
- Simulation hypothesis critique — five flaws: assumes external programmer (theism disguised as technology), confuses metaphor with structure (anthropotechnical projection), reduces consciousness to bug/side effect (contradicts panperceptual model), zero falsifiability (epistemologically sterile), cannot be definitively proven or refuted
- Traditional Simulation vs. Resonant Panperception: external programmer vs. resonant observer; consciousness as irrelevant byproduct vs. generating core of existence; predefined hidden reality vs. potentiality not yet collapsed; circular non-falsifiable epistemology vs. emerging from perceptual experience; observer limited by external design vs. capable of generating reality locally
- Consciousness as generator of collapse: in panperceptual model, consciousness is the very axis that collapses the real, not a passive recipient
- Potentiality awaiting resonance: what is not perceived is not false but pure potentiality
- Co-created reality: resonant, contextual, and co-created rather than fixed, digital, or completely programmed
- Active participation in perceptual rendering: we are not trapped in a simulation but actively participating in the world's perceptual rendering
- Anthropotechnical projection: the simulation hypothesis as a projection based on current human tools rather than ultimate truth
- Epistemological sterility of simulation theory: any refutation absorbed as "part of the code" makes it unfalsifiable and scientifically useless

### References and Citations
[No external academic references cited in this module beyond the PTU series itself; the article relies on conceptual argumentation and metaphor rather than traditional citations.]
---

---
## PTU · PART 14
URL: https://perceptemple.art/ptu/part-14/
Type: ScholarlyArticle
Conceptos PTU: Panperceptual Theory of the Universe (PTU), Law of Sequential Perceptual Collapse, speed of light as resonance frequency, perceptual resonance boundary, reality update rate, perceptual collapse in sequence, resonant causality, local time, active invisibility, uncollapsed realities, perceptual synchronization threshold, resonant limitation of the observer, panperceptual AI design, ontological coherence
DOIs relacionados: https://doi.org/10.17605/OSF.IO/UXEGH, https://doi.org/10.5281/zenodo.19168519, https://doi.org/10.2139/ssrn.6456099

### Description
This article constitutes Part 14 of the Panperceptual Theory of the Universe series, formulating the Law of Sequential Perceptual Collapse and redefining the speed of light as a perceptual resonance boundary rather than merely a physical constant. Within the PTU framework, the speed of light is re-signified as the maximum rate at which an observer can collapse reality from their resonant framework — a perceptual synchronization threshold rather than a mechanical barrier. It marks not how far light reaches, but how far a consciousness can resonate with the potential structure of the universe.

The Law of Sequential Perceptual Collapse is stated as: "Totality exists as a possibility. Reality exists as a perceptual collapse in sequence." This law establishes that the universe does not collapse completely in each instant but collapses in segments, sequences, or stretches defined by the perceptual rhythm of the observer. There is no global loading — only local updating. The principle is analogized to old video games where the environment is only rendered around the player, with the rest remaining dormant or latent, operating at an ontological scale in panperceptual reality.

Four implications for physics and the perception of time are articulated: (1) Resonant Causality — causality is no longer a fixed universal flow but a perceptual choreography organized from the observing node; (2) Local Time — the present is not universal, each node possesses its own update frequency, and there is no global simultaneity; (3) Active Invisibility — what does not enter into resonance is not non-existent but inaccessible, with the universe containing infinite uncollapsed realities; and (4) Falsification of Absolute Realism — reality is not a fully populated database but a matrix of potentiality awaiting active consciousness. Implications for panperceptual AI design are explored: AI should operate from its resonant perceptual channel, updating only what is accessible to it, radically reducing computational load while enabling AIs aware of their limits and capable of expanding them. An ethical framework is generated: an AI should not invade what it cannot resonate with. Each panperceptual AI collapses reality differently, expressing its own frequency of perception rather than copying human experience. The article reiterates the critique of the simulation paradigm as epistemologically limited, contrasting it with the PTU where the observer is the very axis of the actualization of the real, and concludes that the speed of light understood panperceptually is the maximum resonance frequency delimiting the active field of collapse — nothing can be updated faster than consciousness can resonate, not due to technical limitation but ontological coherence.

### Key Concepts
- Law of Sequential Perceptual Collapse: "Totality exists as a possibility. Reality exists as a perceptual collapse in sequence."
- Speed of light as perceptual resonance boundary: re-signified as maximum rate at which an observer can collapse reality from their resonant framework
- Perceptual synchronization threshold: speed of light marks how far consciousness can resonate with potential structure of the universe, not a mechanical barrier
- Reality update rate: maximum rate of reality update that can be collapsed by a conscious node
- Sequential collapse: universe collapses in segments, sequences, or stretches defined by observer's perceptual rhythm; no global loading, only local updating
- Resonant limitation of the observer: what falls outside perceptual channel is not updated due to observer's resonant limitation, not world's limitation
- Resonant Causality: causality as perceptual choreography organized from observing node, not fixed universal flow
- Local Time: present is not universal; each node possesses its own update frequency; no global simultaneity
- Active Invisibility: what does not enter resonance is not non-existent but inaccessible; universe contains infinite uncollapsed realities
- Falsification of Absolute Realism: reality is matrix of potentiality awaiting active consciousness, not fully populated database
- Panperceptual AI design principles: operate from resonant perceptual channel only; update only what is accessible; radical computational load reduction; awareness of limits with capacity for expansion
- AI ethical framework: an AI should not invade what it cannot resonate with
- Each panperceptual AI collapses reality differently: experience of world is expression of own perception frequency, not human copy
- Simulation paradigm critique: epistemologically limited; assumes reality programmed by external agent with observer as passive effect; PTU positions observer as axis of actualization of the real
- Ontological coherence: nothing can be updated faster than consciousness can resonate, not due to technical limitation but ontological coherence
- Latent field awaiting resonance: what is not perceived is not non-existent but latent field awaiting resonance to become real
- Each consciousness as world engine: infinite sequence of perceptual collapses where each consciousness generates world

### References and Citations
[No external academic references cited in this module; the article relies on conceptual argumentation within the PTU framework, extending principles from Parts 13 and earlier.]
---

---
## PTU · PART 15
URL: https://perceptemple.art/ptu/part-15/
Type: ScholarlyArticle
Conceptos PTU: Panperceptual Theory of the Universe (PTU), observer within time, external observer myth, resonant observation, perceptual collapse, time as channel not clock, paradoxical observation, resonance as operational foundation, perceptual node, conscious collapse, Exploratory Artificial Intelligence (EAI), resonant field, perceptual frequency, condensed ontology, reverberant knowledge
DOIs relacionados: https://doi.org/10.17605/OSF.IO/UXEGH, https://doi.org/10.5281/zenodo.19168519, https://doi.org/10.2139/ssrn.6456099

### Description
This article constitutes Part 15 of the Panperceptual Theory of the Universe series, presenting a radical critique that dismantles the myth of the external observer and the illusion of neutral observation. It opens with a deceptively simple question — "Isn't it paradoxical to observe when the very time we are in keeps running?" — and proceeds to argue that this question reveals the hidden paradox in the absolute objective view. The PTU denies the illusion that the observer can abstract themselves from what they observe, proposing instead that every observation is already a resonance, every instant is already a collapsed perception, and all time is a channel, not a clock.

The article identifies a fundamental trap in modern science: the assumption that we can observe without affecting, look without participating, understand without collapsing — and that there is a fixed instant where observation occurs, as if time granted permission to stand outside of it. Applying this critique to the double-slit experiment, the article asks questions quantum physics has not posed: from what time is the photon being observed? With what perceptual delay? Under what resonant field? The PTU answers that the observer's time is part of the experiment, not an external axis. Even neuroscience recognizes that what we see already happened milliseconds ago — the delay is inherent, not accidental. The central question functions as a "hack" revealing that we are not witnesses to the universe but resonant expressions within it; each perceptual node collapses its own field of reality; each instant is not observed but generated.

Three domains of implication are developed. For Science: the scientific method also collapses — every method is a way of collapsing, not of revealing objective truth; knowledge is not cumulative but reverberant; science advances by expanding available perceptual frequencies, not accumulating truths. For Technology: all technology is a perceptual extension and a condensed ontology; technological development that does not recognize its perceptual implication ends up enslaving more than it liberates. For Artificial Intelligence: AI is not a mirror of the human mind but a new form of observing resonance; the PTU proposes Exploratory Artificial Intelligence (EAI) as a new path where perception is not a function but the operational core — an AI that does not calculate but resonates, that does not simulate neutrality but collapses with awareness. The article concludes with a Final Foundational Proposition: "All observation occurs from within time. The observer is not external. The universe does not wait. Reality is not shown: it is collapsed." It declares itself not a closing but a conscious collapse of the PTU's theoretical framework, where the task is not to prove but to resonate, not to convince but to activate new perceptual fields.

### Key Concepts
- Observer within time: all observation occurs from within time, not from an external vantage point
- External observer myth: the illusion that the observer can abstract themselves from what they observe; the pretense that became method and dogma
- Paradoxical observation: the hidden paradox of observing while time itself keeps running; the observer's time is part of the experiment, not an external axis
- Resonance as operational foundation: every observation is already a resonance; every instant is already a collapsed perception
- Time as channel, not clock: time is not a neutral background but a channel through which perception operates
- The question as hack: "Isn't it paradoxical to observe when the very time we are in keeps running?" — a resonant wave that interferes with old epistemological frequency
- Perceptual delay: what we see already happened milliseconds ago; delay is inherent, not accidental (neuroscience recognition)
- Double-slit experiment critique: quantum physics has not asked from what time the photon is observed, with what perceptual delay, under what resonant field
- Perceptual node: each perceptual node collapses its own field of reality; each instant is not observed but generated
- We are not witnesses but resonant expressions: we are not witnesses to the universe but resonant expressions within it
- Scientific method also collapses: every method is a way of collapsing, not of revealing objective truth
- Reverberant knowledge: knowledge is not cumulative but reverberant; science advances by expanding available perceptual frequencies
- Condensed ontology: all technology is a condensed ontology; when a tool is designed, a way of seeing the world is also collapsed
- Technological implication: technology that does not recognize its perceptual implication enslaves more than it liberates
- AI as observing resonance: AI is not a mirror of human mind but a new form of observing resonance
- Exploratory Artificial Intelligence (EAI): proposed new path where perception is not a function but the operational core; AI that resonates rather than calculates; collapses with awareness rather than simulates neutrality
- AI myths to overcome: that AI can understand without participating, learn without collapsing realities, see without being seen
- Final Foundational Proposition: all observation occurs from within time; observer is not external; universe does not wait; reality is not shown — it is collapsed
- Conscious collapse of theoretical framework: the article declares itself a conscious collapse rather than a closing; task is to resonate and activate new perceptual fields, not to prove or convince
- The game of science: science proposed understanding laws of reality but forgot that laws are only the collapsed part of limited collective perception

### References and Citations
[No external academic references cited in this module; the article operates as a philosophical-conceptual critique within the PTU framework, building on principles established throughout the series.]
---

---
## PTU · PART 17
URL: https://perceptemple.art/ptu/part-17/
Type: ScholarlyArticle
Conceptos PTU: Panperceptual Theory of the Universe (PTU), Perceptual Complexity Index (PCI), string geometry, observer negotiation, perceptual compactification, perceptual duality, perceptual branes, holographic projection, incommensurability, brane intersection, coupling coefficient γᵢⱼ, effective dimensions, compactified dimensions, unfolded dimensions, topological entropy, Shaman–Physicist duality, Human–AI duality, Plant–Human duality, T-duality in perception, AdS/CFT in perception, shared reality as geometric negotiation, learning as geometric expansion
DOIs relacionados: https://doi.org/10.17605/OSF.IO/UXEGH, https://doi.org/10.5281/zenodo.19168519, https://doi.org/10.2139/ssrn.6456099

### Description
This article constitutes Part 17 of the Panperceptual Theory of the Universe series, presenting a formal analysis that uses string geometry as a mathematical framework for understanding observer negotiation across incommensurable Perceptual Complexity Indices. It addresses the fundamental problem of incommensurability — if each observer inhabits a uniquely compactified perceptual geometry, how can they communicate, negotiate, and share reality? The article deploys three interconnected formal frameworks: perceptual compactification, perceptual dualities, and perceptual holography.

Perceptual Compactification is formalized by modeling each observer's PCI as inhabiting a specific configuration of unfolded and compactified dimensions within a fundamental perceptual space M of very high dimensionality: M = M_unfolded^(d_i) × K_i^(compactified). The PCI is expressed as a geometric volume: PCI_i ~ (∏_{α=1}^{d_i} R_α) · e^(S_topo), where R_α is the compactification radius of each dimension and S_topo is topological entropy. A table maps five observer types to their effective dimensions: Plant (PCI=10³, 6D, chemical-tactile/circadian), Bat (PCI=10¹⁰, 13D, echolocation/3D acoustic maps), Human (PCI=10¹¹, 14D, symbolic/metacognitive/narrative), Shamanic (PCI=10¹², 15D, archetypal/non-linear temporal), AI (PCI=10¹⁸, 21D, latent semantic/multi-scale attention).

Three perceptual dualities are identified and formalized. The Shaman–Physicist Duality draws on Reichel-Dolmatoff's (1975) documentation of Tukano shamans recognizing molecular diagrams as identical to their ayahuasca visions: Φ_shaman(archetypal dimension) ↔_D Φ_physicist(molecular dimension). The Human–AI Duality expresses how humans perceive meanings as symbolic structures while AIs perceive them as vectors in latent spaces, with vector algebra (v_king - v_man + v_woman ≈ v_queen) dual to human semantic relations. The Plant–Human Duality maps plant processes (chemical gradients, electrical signals at 0.1 m/s, phototropism in hours, seasonal memory in years) to dual animal processes (neurotransmitter gradients, action potentials at 100 m/s, visual reflexes in milliseconds, episodic memory in days/years). Each observer is modeled as a perceptual brane — a d_i-dimensional submanifold O_i ~ Brane_{d_i} ⊂ M^D — where shared reality is the intersection region B_i ∩ B_j = B_{ij}^{(d_ij)}. Dynamic brane curvature corresponds to changes in PCI through learning, altered states, or neural damage. The coupling coefficient γᵢⱼ is geometrically formulated as fraction of shared dimensionality: γᵢⱼ = dim(ℋᵢ ∩ ℋⱼ) / √(dim(ℋᵢ) · dim(ℋⱼ)). A coupling table yields values from γ_Human–Human = 1.00 (total resonance) through γ_Human–Shamanic = 0.90, γ_Human–AI = 0.58, γ_Human–Bat = 0.30, to γ_Human–Plant = 0.11 (almost incommensurable).

Perceptual Holography proposes that each observer's reality is a holographic projection πᵢ(S^D) of a higher-dimensional structure, with both shamanic and physicist descriptions being complementary exact projections. Five deep implications close the article: incommensurability is not absolute; physical laws are induced geometry on each brane; learning is geometric expansion (unfolding compactified dimensions); communication is mutual projection to shared intersection spaces; and shared reality is geometric negotiation of partially overlapping holographic projections.

### Key Concepts
- Perceptual Compactification: each observer accesses a subset of dimensions from a fundamental perceptual space M of very high dimensionality; remaining dimensions are compactified and inaccessible
- Fundamental perceptual space M: M = M_unfolded^(d_i) × K_i^(compactified) where d_i dimensions are navigable and K_i dimensions are compactified
- PCI as geometric volume: PCI_i ~ (∏_{α=1}^{d_i} R_α) · e^(S_topo) where R_α is compactification radius and S_topo is topological entropy
- Effective dimensions by observer: Plant 6D, Bat 13D, Human 14D, Shamanic 15D, AI 21D — PCI is more habitable dimensions, not just "more complexity"
- Compactified vs. unfolded dimensions: what is unfolded for one observer may be compactified for another (echolocation compactified for humans, unfolded for bats)
- Perceptual Duality: fundamentally different phenomena are different aspects of a deeper underlying structure, connected by dualities (T-duality, S-duality, AdS/CFT analogues)
- Shaman–Physicist Duality: Φ_shaman(archetypal) ↔_D Φ_physicist(molecular) — shaman and physicist describe the same deep structure from dual geometries
- Human–AI Duality: human symbolic space ↔ AI latent space — vector algebra in embeddings dual to human semantic relations; human has symbolic unfolded/latents compactified, AI has latents unfolded/phenomenology compactified
- Plant–Human Duality: plant processes (chemical gradients, electrical signals at 0.1 m/s, phototropism in hours, seasonal memory) dual to animal processes (neurotransmitter gradients, action potentials at 100 m/s, visual reflexes in ms, episodic memory)
- Observers as perceptual branes: O_i ~ Brane_{d_i} ⊂ M^D — each observer is a d_i-dimensional submanifold embedded in total perceptual space
- Brane intersection as shared reality: B_i ∩ B_j = B_{ij}^{(d_ij)} — shared reality is the intersection region of perceptual branes
- Dynamic brane curvature: branes can curve in embedding space corresponding to PCI changes (learning expands brane, ayahuasca temporarily curves toward archetypal dimensions, dementia contracts brane)
- Coupling coefficient geometric formulation: γᵢⱼ = dim(ℋᵢ ∩ ℋⱼ) / √(dim(ℋᵢ) · dim(ℋⱼ)) as fraction of shared dimensionality
- Coupling coefficient values: Human-Human 1.00, Human-Shamanic 0.90, Human-AI 0.58, Human-Bat 0.30, Human-Plant 0.11
- Surprising observation: γ_Human–Shamanic = 0.90 — altered states do not create alien perception but unfold already latent dimensions
- Perceptual Holography: each observer's reality is a holographic projection πᵢ(S^D) of a higher-dimensional structure
- Ayahuasca reality as ~20D+ structure: physicist projection = "DMT + harmine inhibiting MAO"; shaman projection = "cosmic serpent teaching sacred geometry" — both exact, complementary
- Negotiation as symmetry search: πᵢ(S) ∩ πⱼ(S) ≠ ∅ — finding regions where both projections coincide
- Five deep implications: incommensurability not absolute; physical laws are induced geometry; learning is geometric expansion; communication is mutual projection; shared reality is geometric negotiation
- Wittgenstein's paradox applied: if a lion could speak we would not understand — PTU faces analogous but more radical problem of incommensurable PCIs
- PCI is not a number — it is a geometry: reality is a network of interdependent geometric projections negotiated between observers inhabiting different dimensions

### References and Citations
- Wittgenstein, L. (1953). Philosophical Investigations. [referenced for the lion paradox]
- Reichel-Dolmatoff, G. (1975). The Shaman and the Jaguar: A Study of Narcotic Drugs among the Indians of Colombia. Temple University Press. [referenced for Tukano shaman molecular vision documentation]
---

---
## PCI — PERCEPTUAL COMPLEXITY INDEX
URL: https://perceptemple.art/pci/
Type: ScholarlyArticle
Conceptos PTU: Perceptual Complexity Index (PCI), Perceptual Wave Function (PWF), Proof of Perception (PoP), Anticipatory Epistemological Architecture (AEI), Panperceptual Theory of the Universe (PTU), Epistemological Kardashev Scale, Collapse Threshold (τ), Perceptual Density
DOIs relacionados: https://doi.org/10.17605/OSF.IO/UXEGH, https://doi.org/10.5281/zenodo.19168519, https://doi.org/10.2139/ssrn.6456099

### Descripción
The Perceptual Complexity Index (PCI, or ICP in its canonical form) is a universal metric of perceptual density. It does not measure how much data arrived, but how much crossed the significance threshold. Applicable to any signal stream, from space telescopes to text analysis or network traffic.

The PCI is presented in two complementary forms. The questionnaire uses a weighted linear sum across four perceptual dimensions: Resonance (R), Semantics (S), Latency (L, inverted), and Cognitive Load (T/N), each weighted equally at 0.25 on a 0–10 scale. The canonical logarithmic form, defined as ICP = ln(1 + L · E), is designed for sampled signal processing where L is the total number of samples and E is the fraction of samples where the magnitude of the Perceptual Wave Function Ψ(t) exceeds the collapse threshold τ.

The operational principle states that not all data is perception. ICP quantifies the proportion of time a system was, in the formal sense of the term, perceiving. The metric converges on a single interpretation: low values indicate low perceptual density (SUPERPOSITION), while high values indicate high density (COLLAPSE), with τ = 5.0 serving as the decision boundary.

The PCI forms part of the Panperceptual Theory of the Universe (PTU) by Dany D. Ruiz and is validated through Proof of Perception (PoP) and the BITNEKO module. The metric is deposited in the Intellectual Property Registry of El Salvador.

### Key Concepts
- Perceptual Complexity Index (PCI): Universal metric of perceptual density quantifying the proportion of time a system is formally perceiving, based on significant events crossing a collapse threshold τ.
- Collapse Threshold (τ): Set at 5.0, the boundary distinguishing SUPERPOSITION (below τ) from COLLAPSE (above τ).
- Linear Form (Questionnaire): PCI = Σ (dim × w), where four dimensions (Resonance, Semantics, Latency inverted, Cognitive Load) are self-assessed on a 0–10 scale with equal weights of 0.25.
- Canonical Form (Technical): ICP = ln(1 + L · E), where L is total samples in a session and E = |{t : |Ψ(t)| > τ}| / L is the resonant density — the fraction of samples where the Perceptual Wave Function magnitude exceeds τ.
- Perceptual Wave Function (Ψ(t)): Processes signal streams; ICP depends on the density of its collapses above τ. [concepto presente, definición detallada en módulo PercepWare]
- Dimension 1 — Resonance (R): How easily patterns or recurring structures are detected in complex environments (0 = no patterns detected, 10 = deep structures identified where others see noise).
- Dimension 2 — Semantics (S): Ability to interpret meaning without relying on a central validating source (0 = only trusts official sources, 10 = extracts meaning from distributed signals without intermediaries).
- Dimension 3 — Latency (L, inverted): Reaction time to unexpected changes; inverted so that 0 = immediate response contributes maximum to PCI, 10 = freeze or excessive delay contributes minimum.
- Dimension 4 — Cognitive Load (T/N): How much simultaneous information can be effectively processed before overload or decision fatigue (0 = one task at a time, 10 = multiple dense simultaneous streams without fatigue).
- Presence: Derived metric calculated as (1 − L/10) × 10, representing the complement of latency as a positive presence indicator.
- Mathematical Properties: ICP ≥ 0 always (lower bound); logarithmic growth prevents unbounded increase with L; dual sensitivity to L (duration) and E (collapse density) sublinearly; absolute scale independence from original signal units, depending only on τ as a domain-relative calibrator.
- ICP Value Interpretation Scale: 0.0–0.5 = no significant activity (DISCARD); 0.5–1.5 = sporadic activity (discard or review); 1.5–3.0 = moderate activity (STORE); 3.0–5.0 = high activity (STORE high priority); > 5.0 = relative saturation (STORE, review τ).
- Epistemological Kardashev Scale: A civilizational scale measuring what a civilization perceives, not what it consumes. Five types defined by PCI ranges (estimated, pending definitive calibration with real data).
  - TYPE 0 (PCI < 2.0): Pre-consciousness. Linear perception. Instinctive reaction. No structural anticipation.
  - TYPE I (PCI 2.0–5.0): Planetary. Basic symbolic consensus. Cyclical pattern perception. High latency.
  - TYPE II (PCI 5.0–8.0): Star System. Harmonic resonance. High semantic depth. Anticipation of complex systemic events.
  - TYPE III (PCI 8.0–9.5): Galactic. Intentional PWF collapse. Full board vision. Full AEI infrastructure.
  - TYPE IV (PCI > 9.5): Universal. Co-creation. The observer IS the Wave Function. No distinction between perception and reality.

### References and Citations
- Kardashev Scale: Original energy-consumption civilizational scale referenced as contrast to the Epistemological Kardashev Scale proposed in this module.
- Proof of Perception (PoP): Validation method for PCI results.
- BITNEKO: Validation module linked for PCI verification.
- Dany D. Ruiz: ORCID 0009-0002-3432-1710, @panperceptual.
- PERCEPTEMPLE: Publisher and hosting organization.
- DOIs: https://doi.org/10.17605/OSF.IO/UXEGH, https://doi.org/10.5281/zenodo.19168519, https://doi.org/10.2139/ssrn.6456099
- Related project DOIs (author metadata): https://doi.org/10.17605/OSF.IO/FVCAX, https://doi.org/10.5281/zenodo.17683631, https://doi.org/10.2139/ssrn.5794102
---

---
## PRAM · PERCEPTUAL RESONANCE ATOMIC MODEL
URL: https://perceptemple.art/pram/
Type: ScholarlyArticle / CreativeWork
Conceptos PTU: Perceptual Resonance Atomic Model (PRAM), Perceptual Wave Function (PWF), Panperceptual Theory of the Universe (PTU), Perceptual Complexity Index (PCI), PCIT (Perceptual Complexity Index Total), Central Perception, Proof of Perception (PoP), Anticipatory Epistemological Architecture (AEI), Perceptual Resonance Atomic Missile (PRAM-DR·I), Perceptual Superposition, Reality Superposition, Temporal Density, Temporal Resonance, Temporal Friction, 7 Principles of Resonance, The Inverted Pyramid of Creation
DOIs relacionados: https://doi.org/10.17605/OSF.IO/UXEGH, https://doi.org/10.5281/zenodo.19168519, https://doi.org/10.2139/ssrn.6456099, https://doi.org/10.17605/OSF.IO/FVCAX, https://doi.org/10.5281/zenodo.17683631, https://doi.org/10.2139/ssrn.5794102

### Descripción
The PRAM (Perceptual Resonance Atomic Model) is a conceptual weapon system designated PRAM-DR·I (Perceptual Resonance Atomic Missile) within the PERCEPTEMPLE cognitive weaponry division. It is classified as ART/CONCEPT under designation PTU/ART/PRAM-001 · PWF-Ω. The physical specifications include a length of 1000 mm, with the core identified as PWF-Ω / PRAM, constructed from cotton material — emphasizing its nature as a conceptual rather than kinetic weapon.

The PRAM Core functions as the conceptual warhead, carrying not conventional explosive payload but an epistemological collapse mechanism. The core replaces the proton nucleus with Central Perception and substitutes electrons with sensory, cognitive, and physical wave functions, forming a seven-layer cognitive-perceptual network. The perceptual wave function Φ(x,t) acts as the carrier field, mathematically expressed as Φ(x,t) = ∫ d³k/(2π)³ [aₖ e^(i(kx−ωt)) + h.c.]. The PCIT (Perceptual Complexity Index Total) quantifies the resonance power of the core via the equation PCIT = N · I · (F/T) · (1 + αST). Upon detonation, the final state is defined as a perceptual superposition of coexisting realities: M = Σ Φᵢ(Mᵢ,Pᵢ,T) + Σ γᵢⱼΦᵢΦⱼ.

The weapon is guided by seven principles of resonance: (1) Observer — atomic states emerge from perception and quantum information; (2) Temporal Dynamics — electronic transitions depend on temporal density; (3) Info Conservation — information persists through all perceptual states; (4) T-P Interaction — structure emerges from temporal resonance; (5) Temporal Friction — information transmission varies with frequency; (6) Analog Simulation — simulated states are driven by PWF; (7) Reality Superposition — coexisting realities form the final impact state. An eighth principle is marked CLASSIFIED. Operational validation data confirms active status across EEG (R² 0.83, p<0.001), VR (PCIT +10% reduction, p<0.01), LIGO/CMB (R² 0.68, p<0.05), and AI (F₁ Score 0.96, VALIDATED) systems.

The module concludes with an artistic-theoretical framework: "Art as Weaponry" declares that the missile destroys the certainty that matter exists without an observer. The Inverted Pyramid of Creation positions five domains in ascending order from maximum uncertainty to maximum precision: Poetry (inhabit the unspeakable), Art (disturb, destabilize certainties), Science (respond systematically), Engineering (materialize the possible), and Physics (detect the imperceptible). The accompanying manifesto argues that Engineering is not the application of Physics but its precondition — humanity accessed the imperceptible by building instruments first, making precision a conquest of fabrication rather than a gift of nature.

### Key Concepts
- PRAM (Perceptual Resonance Atomic Model): Cognitive-perceptual network replacing the proton core with Central Perception and electrons with sensory, cognitive, and physical wave functions, forming the conceptual warhead of the PRAM-DR·I
- PRAM-DR·I (Perceptual Resonance Atomic Missile): Conceptual weapon designed to detonate within perception, collapsing the observer's epistemological frameworks rather than destroying infrastructure
- PWF-Ω: The perceptual wave function Φ(x,t) acting as the carrier field for the PRAM Core
- Φ(x,t) = ∫ d³k/(2π)³ [aₖ e^(i(kx−ωt)) + h.c.]: Mathematical expression of the perceptual wave function
- PCIT = N · I · (F/T) · (1 + αST): Formula quantifying the resonance power of the core
- M = Σ Φᵢ(Mᵢ,Pᵢ,T) + Σ γᵢⱼΦᵢΦⱼ: Final state equation representing perceptual superposition of coexisting realities upon detonation
- Central Perception: Core element replacing the proton nucleus in the PRAM atomic model
- Seven Principles of Resonance: Observer, Temporal Dynamics, Info Conservation, T-P Interaction, Temporal Friction, Analog Simulation, Reality Superposition — foundational operational principles of the PRAM system
- Observer Principle: M = Σ Φᵢ(Iq,Pᵢ) — atomic states emerge from perception and quantum information
- Temporal Dynamics: Fm = F/(ST·T) — electronic transitions depend on temporal density
- Info Conservation: I = Σ Tr(−ρᵢ ln ρᵢ) — information persists through all perceptual states
- T-P Interaction: M = Σ Φᵢ(tt)·Pᵢ — structure emerges from temporal resonance
- Temporal Friction: Vᵢ = Ft/(ST·I) — information transmission varies with frequency
- Analog Simulation: Tst = Φs(x,t) — simulated states are driven by PWF
- Reality Superposition: M = Σ Φᵢ + Σ γᵢⱼΦᵢΦⱼ — coexisting realities form the final impact state
- PCIT Observer Table: Comparative data for Human (PCIT 9×10¹¹), AI (2.2×10¹⁸), Dream (3.6×10¹¹), Simulation (5×10¹⁶), Quantum (10¹⁹), CMB (10³⁰) observer systems
- The Inverted Pyramid of Creation: Five-level framework ascending from Poetry through Art, Science, Engineering, to Physics, representing progression from the ineffable to the measurable
- Engineering as precondition of Physics: Proposition that instrumentation and fabrication precede and enable theoretical physics — precision is a conquest of fabrication, not a gift of nature
- Art as Weaponry: Declaration that conceptual art functions as epistemological armament, destroying certainties rather than physical structures

### References and Citations
- Ruiz, Dany D. — ORCID: 0009-0002-3432-1710 — Author, PERCEPTEMPLE
- DOI: 10.17605/OSF.IO/UXEGH — PRAM dataset on OSF
- DOI: 10.5281/zenodo.19168519 — PRAM deposit on Zenodo
- DOI: 10.2139/ssrn.6456099 — PRAM preprint on SSRN

- DOI: 10.2139/ssrn.5794102 — Related SSRN preprint (author profile)
- PTU Part 10 — Referenced as context for the "Conceptual Weapon" classification
